lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਉ ਪ੍ਰਭਾਤੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਧਿਆਈਐ ਛੋਡਹੁ ਦੁਨੀ ਪਰੀਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: naau prabhaatai sabad dhiaaeeai chhoddahu dunee pareetaa |
lines.english: Meditate on the Name, and the Word of the Shabad, in the early hours before dawn; leave your worldly entanglements behind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਖੇਲ ਕਰੇ ਸਭ ਕਰਤਾ ਐਸਾ ਬੂਝੈ ਕੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: aape khel kare sabh karataa aisaa boojhai koee |3|
lines.english: The Creator Himself plays all the games; only a few understand this. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਮਨੁ ਧਾਇਆ ਮਨੁ ਪੰਖੀ ਆਕਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: man maaeaa man dhaaeaa man pankhee aakaas |
lines.english: The mind is Maya, the mind is a chaser; the mind is a bird flying across the sky.
Prabhaatee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਣਵਤਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸਨਿ ਦਾਸਾ ਜਗਿ ਹਾਰਿਆ ਤਿਨਿ ਜੀਤਾ ॥੪॥੯॥
lines.translit: pranavat naanak daasan daasaa jag haariaa tin jeetaa |4|9|
lines.english: Prays Nanak, the slave of God's slaves: the world loses, and he wins. ||4||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤੂ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਰਾਖਿ ਲੈਹਿ ਸਾਬਤੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਰਾਸਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jaa too raakheh raakh laihi saabat hovai raas |1|
lines.english: Lord, when You save someone, he is saved; his capital is safe and sound. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਸਕਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਨਿਵਾਰਿਆ ਨਗਰੁ ਵੁਠਾ ਸਾਬਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: tasakar sabad nivaariaa nagar vutthaa saabaas |
lines.english: The thieves are overpowered by the Shabad, and then the body-village prospers and celebrates.
Such is my Treasure, the Jewel of the Naam;

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੇਹਿ ਲਗਉ ਪਗਿ ਤੇਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramat dehi lgau pag terai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: please bless me with the Guru's Teachings, so that I may fall at Your Feet. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਜੋਗੀ ਮਨੁ ਭੋਗੀਆ ਮਨੁ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਗਾਵਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: man jogee man bhogeea man moorakh gaavaar |
lines.english: The mind is a Yogi, the mind is a pleasure-seeker; the mind is foolish and ignorant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਦਾਤਾ ਮਨੁ ਮੰਗਤਾ ਮਨ ਸਿਰਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: man daataa man mangataa man sir gur karataar |
lines.english: The mind is the giver, the mind is the beggar; the mind is the Great Guru, the Creator.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਮਾਰਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਐਸਾ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: panch maar sukh paaeaa aisaa braham veechaar |2|
lines.english: The five thieves are conquered, and peace is attained; such is the contemplative wisdom of God. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਏਕੁ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ਕਹਉ ਨ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt ek vakhaaneeai khau na dekhiaa jaae |
lines.english: The One Lord is said to be in each and every heart, but no one can see Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਟੋ ਪੂਠੋ ਰਾਲੀਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਪਤਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: khotto poottho raaleeai bin naavai pat jaae |
lines.english: The false are cast upside-down into the womb of reincarnation; without the Name, they lose their honor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤੂ ਮੇਲਹਿ ਤਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹਾਂ ਜਾਂ ਤੇਰੀ ਹੋਇ ਰਜਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaa too meleh taa mil rahaan jaan teree hoe rajaae |3|
lines.english: Those whom You unite, remain united, if it is Your Will. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਤਿ ਜਨਮੁ ਨਹ ਪੂਛੀਐ ਸਚ ਘਰੁ ਲੇਹੁ ਬਤਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jaat janam neh poochheeai sach ghar lehu bataae |
lines.english: God does not ask about social class or birth; you must find your true home.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਜਾਤਿ ਸਾ ਪਤਿ ਹੈ ਜੇਹੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: saa jaat saa pat hai jehe karam kamaae |
lines.english: That is your social class and that is your status - the karma of what you have done.
He is awake, and even happy, but he is being plundered - he is blind!
Prabhaatee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਦੁਖੁ ਕਾਟੀਐ ਨਾਨਕ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਨਾਇ ॥੪॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: janam maran dukh kaatteeai naanak chhoottas naae |4|10|
lines.english: The pains of death and rebirth are eradicated; O Nanak, salvation is in the Lord's Name. ||4||10||
The noose is around his neck, and yet, his head is busy with worldly affairs.
The strings of his life are all tangled up; he is utterly helpless. ||1||
In hope, he comes and in desire, he leaves.
The Lord of Awareness, the Lord of Life is awake and aware.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿਓ ਨ ਬੂਝੈ ਅੰਧੁ ਨ ਸੂਝੈ ਭੋਂਡੀ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kahio na boojhai andh na soojhai bhonddee kaar kamaaee |
lines.english: He does not understand what he is told; he is blind - he does not see, and so he does his evil deeds.
He is the Ocean of peace, the Treasure of Ambrosial Nectar. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਨੁ ਦਿਨੁ ਆਵੈ ਤਿਲੁ ਤਿਲੁ ਛੀਜੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਘਟਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: din din aavai til til chheejai maaeaa mohu ghattaaee |
lines.english: With the coming of each and every day, his life is wearing away, bit by bit; but still, his heart is attached to Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਰਮੇਸੁਰੁ ਕਰਮੀ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਾਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aape preet prem paramesur karamee milai vaddaaee |2|
lines.english: The Transcendent Lord Himself showers His Love and Affection; by His Grace, He bestows glorious greatness. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਜੀਆ ਦੇਖਿ ਸਮੑਾਲੈ ਸੁਖੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਪੁਰਬਿ ਕਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis jeea dekh samaalai sukh dukh purab kamaaee |
lines.english: Day and night, God watches over and takes care of His living beings; they receive pleasure and pain according to their past actions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਬੂਡੋ ਠਉਰ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਜਬ ਲਗ ਦੂਜੀ ਰਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bin gur booddo tthaur na paavai jab lag doojee raaee |3|
lines.english: Without the Guru, he is drowned, and finds no place of rest, as long as he is caught in duality. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮਹੀਣੁ ਸਚੁ ਭੀਖਿਆ ਮਾਂਗੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਮਿਲੈ ਵਡਾਈ ॥੪॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: karamaheen sach bheekhiaa maangai naanak milai vaddaaee |4|11|
lines.english: Nanak, the unfortunate one, begs for the charity of Truth; please bless him with this glory. ||4||11||
Prabhaatee, First Mehl:
If I talk too much, I miss out on Your Love.
If I remain silent, the world calls me a fool.
The slanderer slanders me, but even so, I love him. ||1||Pause||
Without the Naam, the Name of the Lord, how can I maintain good conduct? ||1||
Such is the falsehood which is plundering the world.
My mistakes and faults will be judged in Your Court.
He realizes the Naam, the Cause of causes, deep within himself.
He alone knows the way, who has been slandered.
Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he is stamped with the Lord's Insignia in His Court.
Calling oneself sublime, one does not become exalted.
I am filthy and polluted; the True Lord is Immaculate and Sublime.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਸੋਈ ਬਿਧਿ ਜਾਣੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jis no nadar kare soee bidh jaanai |2|
lines.english: He alone knows the way, who is blessed by the Lord's Glance of Grace. ||2||
But one who becomes Gurmukh is absorbed in the Name. ||3||
I am blind, deaf, foolish and ignorant,
The self-willed manmukh openly eats the great poison.
